title: Singapore Corporate Income Tax Return (Form C-S, via myTax Portal, Year of Assessment 2026)
description: Prepare and e-File a Singapore-incorporated company's annual Corporate Income Tax Return using Form C-S, the Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore's (IRAS) simplified return for qualifying small companies, through myTax Portal (mytax.iras.gov.sg) for Year of Assessment (YA) 2026. Form C-S is available to a company incorporated in Singapore with annual revenue of S$5 million or below that derives only income taxable at the prevailing 17% Corporate Income Tax rate and is not claiming carry-back of current-year capital allowances/losses, Group Relief, Investment Allowance, or Foreign Tax Credit/tax deducted at source; a qualifying company with revenue of S$200,000 or below may instead opt for the shorter Form C-S (Lite), which is out of scope here (see VERIFICATION.md). A company that does not meet these conditions must file Form C, which requires submission of full financial statements and tax computation and is also out of scope. This document models Form C-S's three parts: Part A (qualifying-condition declarations, return-reviewer particulars, and the financial period), Part B (the tax-adjustment computation from net profit/loss per the financial statements through to total income after donations), and Part C (supporting figures extracted from the financial statements, the capital-allowance/loss/donation shareholding-test declarations, the new start-up company tax exemption qualifying declaration, the trading-stock appropriation/conversion declaration, and the section 10L foreign-asset-disposal-gains declaration), plus the Confirmation Page's declaration and filer/contact-person details. Out of scope for this version: the Enterprise Innovation Scheme's per-activity enhanced deduction/allowance breakdown (Training, Innovation Projects with Partner Institutions, Intellectual Property Rights acquisition/licensing, IP registration, and Singapore-based R&D — Form C-S's line items 44 to 48), the section 14C Research & Development deduction sub-breakdown (line items 38-39), and Form C-S (Lite) and Form C themselves — see VERIFICATION.md for the full scope rationale. It does not submit the return, compute tax payable, or determine any refund/assessment amount — the live myTax Portal and iras.gov.sg are always authoritative.

source.documentRef: IRAS 'Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for Year of Assessment (YA) 2026' (explanatory-notes-to-ya-2026-form-c-s.pdf), corroborated against the IRAS 'User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite)' (user-guide-(tp)_file-form-c-s...pdf, published 5 May 2026) and the 'Overview of Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite)/ Form C' and 'Guidance on Filing Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite)/ Form C' guidance pages

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema authored from three first-party IRAS PDFs fetched directly with no access block (www.iras.gov.sg, HTTP 200, genuine text layers extracted with pdfjs-dist getTextContent — no OCR needed): the 13-page 'Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026' (the item-by-item field guide, cited per-item as 'Item N' in each field's sourceRef), the 41-page 'User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite)' (screenshots-plus-captions walkthrough of the actual myTax Portal wizard, used to confirm each Part's on-screen grouping, the Form Type Selection revenue gate, and the Confirmation Page/filer-declaration flow), and the 'Overview'/'Guidance on Filing' HTML guidance pages (fetched as raw HTML and stripped of markup directly in this sandbox, not summarized by an intermediate small model, per the project's established discipline of not trusting a WebFetch summary alone for field-bearing government pages) for the Form C-S/Form C-S (Lite)/Form C eligibility-threshold table. myTax Portal itself is Singpass/Corppass-authenticated and could not be walked screen-by-screen in this environment (the same constraint as sg/iras/individual-income-tax-return-formb1, sg/ica/passport-application, and sg/acra/sole-proprietorship-registration); VERIFICATION.md includes a worked mock-data walkthrough reproducing the Explanatory Notes' own worked tax-computation example instead, and the conformance packet validates a complete example return against this schema's own required/requiredWhen constraints. Five auto-computed subtotal/carry-forward line items are deliberately NOT modelled as fields, per this registry's existing discipline (e.g. ie/revenue/self-assessment-tax-return-form11s's 'Tax payable... computed by' precedent and the spec's own founder decision deferring calculated/derived fields, SPEC.md §16): Item 5 (Adjusted Profit/Loss before Other Deductions), Item 9 (Adjusted Profit/Loss before Capital Allowances), Item 14c (Net Rental Income), Item 17 (Total Income/Losses before Donations), and Item 20 (Total Income/Losses after Donations) are all listed in the User Guide's own 'will be computed automatically' bullet list and have no on-screen input box. By contrast, Items 11, 13, and 18 (Unutilised Capital Allowances/Losses/Donations brought forward) ARE modelled as fields, because although myTax Portal pre-fills them from IRAS' own record, the Explanatory Notes and User Guide both describe a 'Company's Declaration' box the filer can override if their own records differ — the same discipline used for sg/iras/individual-income-tax-return-formb1's IRAS-pre-filled reliefs, except here an override is possible so the field is retained rather than dropped. Not placed on the edition axis: Singapore's Year of Assessment fits none of the closed edition.scheme vocabulary (us-tax-year/gb-tax-year/award-year) — the same GSP-0019 gap already flagged for sg/iras/individual-income-tax-return-formb1 and four other jurisdictions' individual returns; see spec/proposals/0019-generalize-edition-scheme-calendar-tax-year.md. Scope cuts, each because the source itself frames these as opt-in incentive-scheme elections rather than universal return content ('please complete this section only if the company is making a claim and has met the qualifying conditions' — User Guide, Part B and Part C Enterprise Innovation Scheme steps): (1) Items 44-48, the Enterprise Innovation Scheme's five per-activity qualifying-cost/enhanced-deduction breakdowns (Training, Innovation Projects with Partner Institutions, IPR acquisition, IPR licensing, IP registration, and R&D undertaken in Singapore) — enterpriseInnovationSchemeDeductions and enterpriseInnovationSchemeAllowances (Items 7 and 12) remain in scope as plain optional total-amount fields since they sit inline in Part B's base numbering, but the per-activity Part C detail behind them does not; (2) Items 38-39, the section 14C R&D deduction's Singapore-only and staff-cost-cap sub-breakdown — item37DeductionForRnDExpenditure (Item 37, the total) remains in scope; (3) Form C-S (Lite) and Form C, both structurally distinct forms from Form C-S proper (Lite has only 6 essential fields per IRAS' own comparison; Form C requires full financial-statement/tax-computation submission), deferred to a future document rather than modelled as editions of this one, consistent with this registry's practice of one document per genuinely distinct process. The example calculation in the Explanatory Notes' own 'Computation of Tax Payable Annex' (net profit $150,000 through to tax payable $7,898.15 after the New Start-up Companies exemption and CIT Rebate) is reproduced field-by-field in this document's VERIFICATION.md mock-data test run. Status remains 'draft'. See VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: hasCorppassApproverOrPreparerAccess
fields.label: Do you have Corppass authorisation as 'Approver' or 'Preparer' for 'Corporate Tax (Filing and Applications)'?
fields.description: myTax Portal's File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite) digital service requires the filer to be authorised by the company as an Approver (can submit directly to IRAS) or Preparer (can only submit to an Approver for their review and onward submission) for Corporate Tax filing in Corppass.
fields.sourceRef: User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite), 'How to File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite)', Step 1

fields.name: companyUen
fields.label: Unique Entity Number (UEN)/ Entity ID
fields.description: The company's Unique Entity Number, required together with Singpass to access the File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite) digital service.
fields.sourceRef: User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite), 'How to File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite)', Step 1

fields.name: yearOfAssessment
fields.label: Year of Assessment (YA)
fields.description: The Year of Assessment being filed. Income is assessed on a preceding-year basis: for a company with a 31 Mar financial year-end, the basis period for YA 2026 is 1 Apr 2024 to 31 Mar 2025.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part A — 'The financial period for this return based on the company's financial statements is'

fields.name: revenueForPeriod
fields.label: Revenue for the period
fields.description: The company's gross revenue for the financial period, entered at the 'Form Type Selection' page before Form C-S itself. It is compulsory and determines which return the filer may use: if revenue exceeds S$5 million, Form C-S is not offered and Form C must be filed; if revenue is S$200,000 or below, the filer may additionally choose the shorter Form C-S (Lite) (out of scope for this document) instead of Form C-S. This document targets the S$200,000-to-S$5,000,000 band, where Form C-S's Part A is shown directly.
fields.sourceRef: User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite), 'Form Type Selection', Steps 1-2 and Notes 1-3

fields.name: financialPeriodStartDate
fields.label: Financial period — start date
fields.description: Start of the financial period this return is based on. myTax Portal pre-fills this from IRAS' record; amend it if it differs from the company's financial statements.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part A — 'The financial period for this return based on the company's financial statements is'

fields.name: financialPeriodEndDate
fields.label: Financial period — end date
fields.description: End of the financial period this return is based on. myTax Portal pre-fills this from IRAS' record; amend it if it differs from the company's financial statements.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part A — 'The financial period for this return based on the company's financial statements is'

fields.name: functionalCurrency
fields.label: Functional currency
fields.description: The currency the company reports in. Most companies report in Singapore dollars (SGD); a non-SGD functional currency changes how certain pre-filled donation figures (Items 19a/19b) may be adjusted. Defaults to SGD if not otherwise stated.
fields.sourceRef: User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite), 'Functional Currency & Contact Information', Step 1; Explanatory Notes Item 19a — 'Upward adjustments are permitted only for companies that use a non-Singapore dollar functional currency'

fields.name: qualifiesRevenueThreshold
fields.label: Qualifying Condition 1: Company's revenue for the financial period is S$5 million or below
fields.description: Revenue is the gross amount of income derived by the company from its principal activities in the basis period for the YA, excluding separate source income such as interest. For an investment holding company, revenue refers to its investment income.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part A, Note 1

fields.name: qualifiesOnlyStandardRateIncome
fields.label: Qualifying Condition 2: Company derives only income subject to tax at 17%
fields.description: The company must not derive tax-exempt income (except one-tier exempt Singapore dividends and/or specified foreign-sourced income exempted under section 13(8)) or income taxable at concessionary tax rates. A company in a tax-loss position may still file Form C-S if it meets the rest of the qualifying conditions.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part A, Note 2

fields.name: qualifiesNoExcludedClaims
fields.label: Qualifying Condition 3: Company is not claiming carry-back of current-year capital allowances/losses, Group Relief, Investment Allowance, or Foreign Tax Credit/tax deducted at source
fields.description: Combines four exclusions (Notes 3a-3d): carry-back of current-year unutilised capital allowances/trade losses against a prior YA's assessable income; Group Relief surrender to another group company; Investment Allowance for approved capital expenditure; and Foreign Tax Credit or Section 45 tax deducted at source.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part A, Notes 3a-3d

fields.name: returnReviewedByAccreditedTaxProfessional
fields.label: Was the return reviewed by a Singapore Chartered Tax Professionals Limited (SCTP) Accredited Tax Advisor, Accredited Tax Practitioner, or Accredited Tax Practitioner (Provisional) for Income Tax?
fields.description: A reviewer can be a tax agent's staff or an in-house tax professional whose role is to review the return's corporate income tax treatment before submission. Where there are multiple accredited reviewers, provide the details of any one reviewer.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part A — 'Particulars of Person who Reviewed the Return'

fields.name: reviewerName
fields.label: Reviewer's name
fields.description: Name of the SCTP-accredited reviewer.
fields.sourceRef: User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite), Part A, 'Particulars of Person who Reviewed the Return', Note 1

fields.name: reviewerScptMembershipNumber
fields.label: Reviewer's SCTP membership number
fields.description: The reviewer's Singapore Chartered Tax Professionals Limited membership number.
fields.sourceRef: User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite), Part A, 'Particulars of Person who Reviewed the Return', Note 1

fields.name: netProfitOrLossBeforeTax
fields.label: Item 1: Net Profit/ Loss before Tax as per Financial Statements
fields.description: Total Income less Total Expenses, extracted from the Statement of Comprehensive Income (Profit & Loss Statement). Pre-filled with '0'; enter the correct amount if not '0'.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 1

fields.name: separateSourceIncome
fields.label: Item 2: Separate Source Income
fields.description: Total income other than from the company's principal activity or trade, e.g. rent from real property, fixed deposit interest, and other non-trade taxable income (detailed further in Items 14a, 15, and 16). Excludes exempt one-tier dividend income, which belongs in Item 3.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 2

fields.name: nonTaxableIncome
fields.label: Item 3: Non-Taxable Income
fields.description: Examples: exempt one-tier dividend income, section 13(8) exempt income, gain on disposal of fixed assets, and specifically exempted receipts such as Jobs Support Scheme and Enterprise Innovation Scheme cash payouts.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 3

fields.name: nonTaxDeductibleExpenses
fields.label: Item 4: Non-Tax Deductible Expenses
fields.description: Expenses not wholly and exclusively incurred in the production of income, not revenue in nature, or prohibited under the Income Tax Act 1947 — e.g. depreciation, donations, fines and penalties, private expenses, S-plate car expenses.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 4

fields.name: renovationRefurbishmentDeductionS14N
fields.label: Item 6: Deduction for Renovation or Refurbishment Works under Section 14N
fields.description: Qualifying renovation/refurbishment expenditure (e.g. general electrical installation and lighting, doors, windows, fixed partitions, flooring, wall covering, and from YA 2025 designer/professional fees) not involving structural changes requiring Commissioner of Building Control approval, subject to a S$300,000 cap for every fixed 3-year period (first period YA 2025-YA 2027).
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 6

fields.name: enterpriseInnovationSchemeDeductions
fields.label: Item 7: Enhanced Deductions under Enterprise Innovation Scheme (EIS)
fields.description: Total enhanced deductions claimed under the EIS (available YA 2024-2028) across qualifying activities: training, innovation projects with partner institutions, acquisition/licensing of IP rights, registration of IP, and qualifying R&D undertaken in Singapore. The per-activity qualifying-cost breakdown (Items 44-48) is out of scope for this document — complete it only if making a claim; see VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 7

fields.name: item8FurtherOrOtherDeductions
fields.label: Item 8: Further Deductions/ Other Deductions
fields.description: Expenditure qualifying for further deductions not included in Items 1 or 6 — e.g. IP protection costs (section 14A), approved trade fairs/exhibitions (section 14B), R&D expenditure (section 14D(1)), overseas investment development (section 14H), IP in-licensing (section 14U), and pre-commencement expenditure (section 14R).
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 8

fields.name: balancingCharge
fields.label: Item 10: Balancing Charge
fields.description: Arises when a fixed asset on which capital allowances were previously claimed is sold or ceases to be used, and the sale proceeds exceed the tax written-down value; restricted to the capital allowances previously allowed on the asset.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 10

fields.name: unutilisedCapitalAllowancesBroughtForward
fields.label: Item 11: Unutilised Capital Allowances brought forward (IRAS' record)
fields.description: Pre-filled by myTax Portal based on the amount IRAS carried forward for the preceding YA. Declare a different amount in the Company's Declaration box if the company's own records differ.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 11

fields.name: currentYearCapitalAllowances
fields.label: Item 12: Current Year Capital Allowances
fields.description: Capital allowances on plant/machinery used in the trade or business, including allowances on hire-purchase equipment, previously-deferred allowances now claimed, EIS enhanced allowances (see Item 7), industrial building allowances, land intensification allowances, IP rights writing-down allowances, and balancing allowances.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 12

fields.name: unutilisedLossesBroughtForward
fields.label: Item 13: Unutilised Losses brought forward (IRAS' record)
fields.description: Pre-filled by myTax Portal based on the amount IRAS carried forward for the preceding YA. Declare a different amount in the Company's Declaration box if the company's own records differ.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 13

fields.name: grossRentalIncome
fields.label: Item 14a: Gross Rental Income
fields.description: Total rental income for the financial period.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 14a

fields.name: deductibleRentalExpenses
fields.label: Item 14b: Less — Deductible Expenses
fields.description: Revenue expenses directly incurred to earn the rental income, e.g. loan interest, agent's commission, insurance, property tax, repair and maintenance. Excludes industrial building allowances and land intensification allowances (Item 12).
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 14b

fields.name: interestIncome
fields.label: Item 15: Interest Income
fields.description: Enter '0' if the amount is negative, as expenses in excess of interest income are disregarded.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 15

fields.name: otherTaxableIncome
fields.label: Item 16: Other Taxable Income
fields.description: Enter '0' if the amount is negative, as expenses in excess of other income are disregarded.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 16

fields.name: unutilisedDonationsBroughtForward
fields.label: Item 18: Unutilised Donations brought forward (IRAS' record)
fields.description: Pre-filled based on the amount IRAS carried forward for the preceding YA, less any donations from the fifth-preceding YA that have expired. Declare a different amount if the company's own records differ.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 18

fields.name: currentYearDonations
fields.label: Item 19a: Current Year Donations (IRAS' record)
fields.description: Pre-filled from current-year qualifying local donations to approved Institutions of a Public Character, auto-included from those institutions. Upward adjustment is only permitted for companies using a non-SGD functional currency.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 19a

fields.name: currentYearOverseasDonations
fields.label: Item 19b: Current Year Overseas Donations (IRAS' record)
fields.description: Tax deduction for qualifying overseas donations under the Overseas Humanitarian Assistance Tax Deduction Scheme (OHAS) and/or the Philanthropy Tax Incentive Scheme for Family Offices (PTIS), capped at 40% of statutory income if it would otherwise exceed that.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part B, Item 19b

fields.name: revenue
fields.label: Item 24: Revenue
fields.description: The company's main source of income (for an investment holding company, its investment income, e.g. dividend or interest income). Pre-filled from the amount entered at the Form Type Selection page; revise it here if needed.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 24

fields.name: grossProfitOrLoss
fields.label: Item 25: Gross Profit/ Loss
fields.description: Revenue less cost of goods sold (COGS). If COGS is nil, gross profit equals revenue.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 25

fields.name: directorsFeesAndRemuneration
fields.label: Item 26: Directors' Fees and Remuneration
fields.description: All fees and payments made to directors — salaries, leave pay, commissions, bonuses, gratuities, allowances, CPF. Excludes benefits-in-kind, medical expenses, and cash allowances in lieu of medical expenses.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 26

fields.name: totalRemunerationExcludingDirectorsFees
fields.label: Item 27: Total Remuneration excluding Directors' Fees
fields.description: Total employees' remuneration (salaries, leave pay, commissions, bonuses, gratuities, allowances, CPF) excluding Directors' Fees, benefits-in-kind, medical expenses, and cash allowances in lieu of medical expenses.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 27

fields.name: medicalExpenses
fields.label: Item 28: Medical Expenses
fields.description: Deductible medical expenses (including cash allowance in lieu of medical expenses and medical insurance), capped at 1% of total employees' remuneration (2% if qualifying inpatient medical benefit conditions are met).
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 28

fields.name: transportTravellingExpenses
fields.label: Item 29: Transport/ Travelling Expenses
fields.description: Transport/travelling expenses as per the financial statements.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 29

fields.name: entertainmentExpenses
fields.label: Item 30: Entertainment Expenses
fields.description: Entertainment expenses as per the financial statements.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 30

fields.name: inventories
fields.label: Item 31: Inventories
fields.description: Also known as stocks/goods. Enter at gross if available in the financial statements, otherwise net of provision.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 31

fields.name: tradeReceivables
fields.label: Item 32: Trade Receivables
fields.description: Also known as trade debtors/accounts receivables. Enter at gross if available in the financial statements, otherwise net of provision.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 32

fields.name: changeInPrincipalActivities
fields.label: Item 33: Change in principal activities
fields.description: To deduct unutilised capital allowances brought forward, there must not be a change in the company's principal activities during the basis period. Select 'N.A.' equivalent (false/not claimed) if unutilised amounts brought forward are not claimed against current-YA income.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 33

fields.name: substantialChangeInShareholders
fields.label: Item 34: Substantial change in the company's ultimate shareholders and their shareholdings as at the relevant dates
fields.description: Unutilised capital allowances/losses/donations can only be deducted against current-year income if at least 50% of shareholdings are held by the same ultimate shareholders as at the relevant dates (the 'shareholding test').
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 34

fields.name: waiverOfShareholdingTestApplied
fields.label: Item 35: Waiver of the shareholding test
fields.description: Where there is a substantial change in shareholders, the company may apply to the Comptroller for a waiver of the shareholding test under sections 23(5), 37(16), 37D(15), or 37O(22) of the Income Tax Act 1947, if the change is not tax-motivated.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 35

fields.name: claimsStartupTaxExemption
fields.label: Item 36: Tax Exemption Scheme for New Start-up Companies — qualifying conditions met
fields.description: The scheme provides tax exemption for a new start-up's first 3 consecutive YAs (75% exemption on the first S$100,000 of normal chargeable income, 50% on the next S$100,000), if the company is incorporated in Singapore, is Singapore tax-resident for the YA, and its total share capital is beneficially held directly by no more than 20 shareholders (all individuals, or at least one individual holding at least 10% of issued ordinary shares). Investment-holding and property-development companies incorporated after 25 February 2013 are not eligible (but may claim partial tax exemption instead).
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 36

fields.name: hasTradingStockAppropriationOrConversion
fields.label: Item 40: Appropriation of trading stock for non-trade/capital purposes (Section 10J) and/or conversion of a non-trade/capital asset to trading stock (Section 32A)
fields.description: A change of use of trading stock occurs when stock intended for sale is instead used for other purposes (e.g. retained as a fixed asset); a conversion occurs when a non-trade/capital asset is subsequently converted to trading stock for sale. If yes, the company must submit the AC Reporting Form via the Submit Document digital service — see the acReportingForm document entry.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 40

fields.name: foreignAssetDisposalGains10L
fields.label: Item 41a: Gains/ losses from the sale or disposal of foreign assets (Section 10L)
fields.description: Whether the company is a covered entity under section 10L that disposed of/sold a foreign asset during the basis period. Select 'Not Applicable' if foreign-sourced disposal gains (FSDGs) are derived solely from foreign intellectual property rights, or if the company is not a covered entity under section 10L.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 41

fields.name: foreignAssetDisposalGainsAmount
fields.label: Item 41b: Amount of foreign-sourced disposal gains (FSDGs) derived during the basis period
fields.description: The amount of foreign-sourced disposal gains derived during the basis period for the current YA. May be left as 0 if the entity is excluded under section 10L(8) and Item 41a is 'No'.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 41

fields.name: foreignAssetDisposalGainsRemitted
fields.label: Item 41c: Amount of FSDGs remitted to Singapore during the basis period
fields.description: The amount of foreign-sourced disposal gains remitted to Singapore during the basis period for the current YA.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 41

fields.name: item37DeductionForRnDExpenditure
fields.label: Item 37: Deduction for expenditure incurred on R&D (undertaken in Singapore and Overseas) — Total deduction under Section 14C
fields.description: Total expenditure (net of government/statutory board grants) incurred on R&D activities conducted wholly in Singapore (in-house or outsourced), wholly overseas (related to trade), mixed Singapore/overseas activities, or under a cost-sharing agreement. The Singapore-only and staff-cost-cap sub-breakdown (Items 38-39) is out of scope for this document; see VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, Part C, Item 37

fields.name: declarationConfirmed
fields.label: Declaration
fields.description: Confirms the Confirmation Page's declaration that the information entered is correct, before submission.
fields.sourceRef: User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite), 'Confirmation Page [Form C-S]', Step 1

fields.name: filerName
fields.label: Filer & Contact Person — name
fields.description: Name of the person completing and submitting the filing.
fields.sourceRef: User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite), 'Confirmation Page [Form C-S]', Step 2

fields.name: filerDesignation
fields.label: Filer & Contact Person — designation
fields.description: The filer's position/designation at the company (e.g. Director, Finance Manager, tax agent staff).
fields.sourceRef: User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite), 'Confirmation Page [Form C-S]', Step 2

fields.name: filerContactNumber
fields.label: Filer & Contact Person — contact number
fields.description: Contact telephone number for the filer.
fields.sourceRef: User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite), 'Confirmation Page [Form C-S]', Step 2

fields.name: filerEmailAddress
fields.label: Filer & Contact Person — email address
fields.description: Contact email address for the filer.
fields.sourceRef: User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite), 'Confirmation Page [Form C-S]', Step 2

fields.name: submissionRole
fields.label: Submission role
fields.description: An Approver submits the return directly to IRAS. A Preparer can only 'Submit to Approver', who must then review and submit it; a Preparer's submission is retained in the Portal (pending the Approver's action) for 21 days or until the filing due date of 30 Nov, whichever is later.
fields.sourceRef: User Guide (Company): File Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite), 'Confirmation Page [Form C-S]', Step 3

documents.label: Financial statements
documents.sourceRef: Overview of Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite)/ Form C — 'If your company qualifies to file Form C-S, it is not required to file its financial statements and tax computation together with Form C-S. However, your company should prepare these documents and be ready to submit them upon IRAS' request.'

documents.sourceRef: Overview of Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite)/ Form C — 'your company should prepare these documents and be ready to submit them upon IRAS' request'
